Those are really the only two types of actual video ads that we create here. Now as a general rule of thumb, all right, number one, you want to have an easy to remember domain name that you're referring people to and these, and you want to make that domain name visible in the ads, no matter what kind of ad it is. You want them to be able to see that domain name all the time. And the reason why is even if they don't click on your ads, they will remember the domain name and they'll type it in. And I've got the analytics to back that up. So you can run ads on YouTube, but vary a lot of different ways. Even though there's only two types of ads, you can actually run them different ways. For example, you can create conversion ads. Now the videos aren't going to be any different, but the way you tell YouTube to handle the ads is different. That's where you say, YouTube, go and get me a lead or a customer or whatever. You can also run the same campaign, same type of video. to be using conversion ads for our work together. So the most important things to consider here when you're creating a campaign on YouTube is number one, who do you show your ads to? And that's the audience. And there's a couple types of audience. So the first one is going to be YouTube generated audiences. These are people that YouTube or Google, I'm going to use the two of them interchangeably because Google owns YouTube. This is where YouTube will be like, oh yeah, you're looking for people who are interested in advertising agencies. Well, we have some of those. And that's called an in market interest audience right here. YouTube also can generate audiences for you based on keywords and they can also generate audiences based on who people are interested in and things like that. So these are YouTube generated audiences and then there is retargeting audiences and this is still following under the category of who. And retargeting doesn't just mean people who visited your website. It also means people who watched your other stuff. So if people watch one of your YouTube videos, you can say only show this ad that people who watched that other one. And that's very, very powerful, very, very helpful. And of course, we can also retarget people who went to your website. So with the kind of blueprint out everything, there are two really simple processes that I like to use on YouTube. Number one is we have content ads that we show to a really big audience like an in market audience, for example, or a custom intent audience that I'll show you how to create when we get to that part of this week's training. So we show those videos to people and then if they watch them, then we show them some more content and we start showing a bunch of offers. And that's really, really straightforward. And one strategy is to go offer and if they watch that offer, then you show them content. And if they watch that content, then you show them an offer and you kind of stack it that way. So if you go back to the old days of internet marketing, it would be like, hey, we want to get everybody to watch our stuff because we want to get known. Well, that's great. But if you're paying for them to watch the video, you want them to be disinterested immediately if they're not your good prospects.

## [06:01 - 09:05]

So in our content, we come right out and we say who it's for. There's a little framework that I'll give you. In fact, I'll give it to you now. This is a, I call it the simple three minute content video framework, even though I have yet to make a content video that's under like four and a half minutes, but whatever. It doesn't have to be a three minute content video, but it doesn't really matter how long your content video is. Here's your framework, right? So number one is you call out to the viewer and you're basically stating who the video is for like, hey, if you have aching joints and you're over 50 years old, and you're a vegetarian, watch this. That's the if you. The do you would be a question. Like do you have aching joints and happen to be a vegetarian? And do you happen to be over 50? If so, then we go into the next step. So that's two simple ways to call out to your market right there. Then the next step here is preview. And this is where you just tell them what you're going to do in this video. So you literally say, like, Hey, in this video, we're going to talk about how to overcome joint pain by eating this one simple food or whatever. Now right after that preview, before you go into the content, you make your offer. You say, and by the way, if you want to take a deeper dive, go to website.com.
